title: Eternal Moonshine Of The Simpson Mind

jokes.text: The capital of montana is not "hannah"
jokes.setup: Chalkboard gag referencing common geography mistake
jokes.punchline: Hannah Montana/Montana state capital confusion
jokes.explanation: Timely Hannah Montana reference with educational twist. Clean structure but relies on pop culture reference.

jokes.text: Nobody steals Willie's breakfast!
jokes.setup: Willie's territorial nature over something mundane
jokes.punchline: Disproportionate anger over breakfast theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie character integration - his accent and protective nature over trivial things.

jokes.text: I must've really tied one on last night.
jokes.setup: Homer waking up confused
jokes.punchline: Classic drunk morning regret line
jokes.explanation: Clichéd but character-appropriate. Sets up the episode premise efficiently.

jokes.text: No more drinking.
jokes.setup: Homer's morning-after resolution
jokes.punchline: Empty promise we know he'll break immediately
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective irony - audience knows Homer's track record with resolutions.

jokes.text: 6:00 A.M. Still early enough to sneak into bed and pretend I actually got home at 3:00 A.M.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to cover his tracks
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous logic that 6 AM could pass for 3 AM
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusional reasoning is perfectly in character and genuinely funny logic.

jokes.text: Or yourselves?
jokes.setup: Homer asking kids where their mother is
jokes.punchline: Realizes the kids themselves are also missing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with perfect economy. The delayed realization is expertly timed.

jokes.text: they must all be taking a family bath. Without me!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to rationalize family's absence
jokes.punchline: Absurd assumption followed by hurt feelings about exclusion
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike disappointment at imaginary exclusion is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: Show me on MapQuest. Fine. Google Maps.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Santa's Little Helper to locate family
jokes.punchline: Switching to more current mapping service when dog doesn't respond
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural reference with good timing. The brand pivot is unexpectedly funny.

jokes.text: Look, if this is about me eating your heart pills, they shouldn't have made them in Good 'N' Plenty colors.
jokes.setup: Homer defending himself against attacking dog
jokes.punchline: Blaming candy-like appearance of medication for eating it
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Homer logic - specific candy reference makes it funnier and more believable.

jokes.text: A 'Forget-Me-Shot?' Never heard of it. Yeah, that means it worked.
jokes.setup: Homer doesn't remember the drink name
jokes.punchline: Moe points out this proves the memory wipe worked
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic that's both funny and plot-functional. Perfect structure and economy.

jokes.text: You start with a splash of Jagermeister... then add sloe gin... Triple-Sec... Quadruple-Sec...
jokes.setup: Moe listing drink ingredients
jokes.punchline: Escalating from Triple-Sec to Quadruple-Sec
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You stir it with a home pregnancy test till it turns positive...
jokes.setup: Describing drink preparation method
jokes.punchline: Using pregnancy test as stirrer with impossible result
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and original. The logical impossibility makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Jeez, I don't look like that.
jokes.setup: Homer seeing himself in Moe's demonstration video
jokes.punchline: Denial about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Standard vanity joke but delivered with Homer's characteristic self-delusion.

jokes.text: I was trying to do a Don Rickles about Arabs, but it turned into a Mel Gibson about Mexicans.
jokes.setup: Patron explaining his comedy mistake
jokes.punchline: Escalating from insult comedy to hate speech references
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural references with perfect escalation structure. Edgy but earned.

jokes.text: Huh? What the hell am I doing here? I gotta get back to the Latin Grammys.
jokes.setup: Same patron after taking the shot
jokes.punchline: Memory wiped, now needs to return to award show
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the drink works, with Latin Grammys reference adding context to his earlier mistake.

jokes.text: There he is! Get him! Kill him! Hot-sauce his eyes!
jokes.setup: Crowd chasing the patron
jokes.punchline: Escalating from kill to oddly specific hot-sauce torture
jokes.explanation: The hot-sauce threat is unexpectedly specific and funnier than generic violence.

jokes.text: Everything's fine cupcakes and sprinkles.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince Chief Wiggum everything is normal
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical phrase meant to sound cheerful
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish attempt at sounding innocent is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: All right, door! You're coming downtown!
jokes.setup: Wiggum hearing 'walked into a door' excuse
jokes.punchline: Arresting the door as the perpetrator
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum moment - brilliant absurdist logic delivered with complete sincerity. Iconic line.

jokes.text: Not that I am authorized to release that information... Flanders! Fine, it was Flanders.
jokes.setup: Wiggum claiming he can't release information
jokes.punchline: Immediately caves when Homer guesses correctly
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum incompetence - good structure with character consistency.

jokes.text: Now, since you know everything, who was Jack the Ripper? The queen's private surgeon!
jokes.setup: Wiggum sarcastically asking about famous mystery
jokes.punchline: Homer confidently provides conspiracy theory answer
jokes.explanation: Homer's confident wrong answer plays well against Wiggum's sarcasm.

jokes.text: I heard a hubbub, Bub.
jokes.setup: Flanders explaining why he called police
jokes.punchline: Rhyming folksy expression
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders speech pattern - wholesome and slightly irritating as intended.

jokes.text: I can't say for sure, but as a Christian, I assume the worst.
jokes.setup: Homer asking what he did wrong
jokes.punchline: Flanders' self-contradictory Christian judgment
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of religious hypocrisy delivered perfectly through Flanders' character.

jokes.text: If only these walls could talk. People would pay to come see my amazing talking walls, and I could use that money to...
jokes.setup: Standard 'if these walls could talk' expression
jokes.punchline: Homer gets distracted by business opportunity instead of the mystery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely misses the point in favor of get-rich-quick scheme.

jokes.text: You know, doing this is just confirming a stereotype for you Mexicans. I am from Costa Rica! And I should care because?
jokes.setup: Worker correcting Homer's nationality assumption
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive ignorance of the distinction
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual ignorance is in character but treads close to cheap stereotype humor.

jokes.text: You'll eat our cookies, but you won't look at us!
jokes.setup: Nursing home glee club performing for residents
jokes.punchline: Frustrated outburst about audience indifference
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor about performative charity and audience discomfort.

jokes.text: That's like asking your horse to do your taxes, which I did back in 1998.
jokes.setup: Grandpa comparing Homer's request to something impossible
jokes.punchline: Reveals he actually tried the impossible comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grandpa Simpson moment - specific year makes the absurdity funnier and more believable.

jokes.text: Punch that orderly who takes sips out of my juice. No, wait. That's the guy that saved my life.
jokes.setup: Grandpa asking Homer to punch someone as payment
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal upon remembering the context
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Grandpa's faulty memory driving the reversal.

jokes.text: How about The Deja View-Master? Remembrance of things fast? The Remembererer?
jokes.setup: Frink struggling to name his memory device
jokes.punchline: Series of increasingly awkward portmanteau attempts
jokes.explanation: Classic Frink overthinking with literary references and made-up words.

jokes.text: Ah, my first Christmas. Such great Chinese food.
jokes.setup: Patient reliving cherished Christmas memory
jokes.punchline: Jewish Christmas tradition reference
jokes.explanation: Standard cultural reference joke. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: To me, 'nerd' stands for Not Even Remotely Dorky, so thank you.
jokes.setup: Homer calling Frink a nerd
jokes.punchline: Frink's positive reinterpretation of the insult
jokes.explanation: Typical Frink defense mechanism with clever acronym twist.

jokes.text: Ah, it's got that new bubble smell.
jokes.setup: Homer entering the memory bubble
jokes.punchline: Treating sci-fi device like a new car
jokes.explanation: Homer's mundane reaction to extraordinary technology is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Now remember-steer into the sculptures.
jokes.setup: Homer giving driving advice during snow sculpture contest
jokes.punchline: Encouraging destructive behavior for fun
jokes.explanation: Homer's bad parenting disguised as fun activity advice.

jokes.text: on top of my TV Guide?!
jokes.setup: Homer listing violations of his marriage: another man, with his wife, in his house
jokes.punchline: Most outraged by the trivial TV Guide detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure with Homer's misplaced priorities creating brilliant anticlimax.

jokes.text: Someone like Bart, only smart. Lisa!
jokes.setup: Homer needing smart help
jokes.punchline: Describing Lisa as 'Bart, only smart'
jokes.explanation: Homer's backhanded compliment to Lisa while inadvertently insulting Bart.

jokes.text: You're getting this memory mixed up with a commercial you saw. With new Synergy Wireless, night time minutes start at 6:30...
jokes.setup: Lisa correcting Homer's false memory
jokes.punchline: Launches into actual commercial with perfect corporate speak
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke about false memories vs. advertising, though slightly long for the payoff.

jokes.text: You're superior to me in every way! Well, let's see how you do against 20-year-old Homer.
jokes.setup: Present Homer losing fight to 10-year-old self
jokes.punchline: Brings in even younger self as backup
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation with Homer's mismatched confidence levels.

jokes.text: Dad, you just destroyed your first kiss. Who was it with? Uh... Apu.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about Homer's first kiss after he disrupts the memory
jokes.punchline: Homer's embarrassed admission of kissing Apu
jokes.explanation: Unexpected answer with perfect delivery of Homer's shame about it.

jokes.text: We're not really your kids. We're just representations of them that you created in your mind.
jokes.setup: Homer wondering if he should show kids adult content
jokes.punchline: Memory Lisa breaks fourth wall about being mental constructs
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on the device's nature, though slightly exposition-heavy.

jokes.text: Hello. I'd like to order a pizza. 35 minutes?!
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he can create anything in his mind
jokes.punchline: Still gets frustrated with normal delivery times in imagination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectations - Homer's limitations persist even in imagination. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Oh, can that tuna! We can get your brain to work the same way you always get the TV to work --by hitting it.
jokes.setup: Frink's technical explanation about memory
jokes.punchline: Reduces solution to Homer's typical fix-it method
jokes.explanation: Good analogy between brain function and Homer's mechanical solutions.

jokes.text: We can get your brain to work the same way you always get the TV to work --by hitting it.
jokes.setup: Need to fix Homer's memory
jokes.punchline: Doctor suggests percussive maintenance like old TVs
jokes.explanation: Good analogy between brain function and old technology repair methods

jokes.text: I'm just giving it to your wife. She is gonna be sore tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Duffman's seemingly sexual statement
jokes.punchline: Will be revealed as innocent party context
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre setup for the misdirection reveal later.

jokes.text: Well, if you can't stand the neural activity, stay out of the neural activator!
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about the device's effects
jokes.punchline: Frink's kitchen-saying parody with scientific terms
jokes.explanation: Classic Frink scientific wordplay on common expression. Well-executed but predictable.

jokes.text: Hey, aren't trolls supposed to be under the bridge? Ha, ha, good-bye.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning troll positioning on bridge
jokes.punchline: Trolls awkwardly acknowledge the folklore error and leave
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke about fairy tale logic with good character interaction.

jokes.text: We were planning a surprise party for you. A surprise party?! This magic marker's running out of ink.
jokes.setup: Major plot revelation about surprise party
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately distracted by mundane marker problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - missing emotional significance for trivial concern.

jokes.text: This is gonna be the greatest party ever! Oh, yeah! It's like Christmas in December.
jokes.setup: Homer's excitement about the party
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to Christmas... in December (when Christmas actually is)
jokes.explanation: Homer's redundant comparison shows his excitement overwhelming his logic.

jokes.text: I must be the first gullible husband to overhear snippets of surprise party planning and think it was my wife having an affair.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on his misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically claiming to be unique in this common sitcom trope
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on sitcom clichés while maintaining character voice.

jokes.text: Blue pants make you look fat. I've never learned your kids' names. Your yard is unkempt. It is obvious you're bald. Your thighs make noise. I find your small hands attractive. I've spit in every drink I ever served you.
jokes.setup: Friends confessing things Homer won't remember
jokes.punchline: Escalating from mild to insulting to disturbing to Moe's gross revelation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with perfect character-specific insults building to Moe's disgusting revelation.

jokes.text: No doubt I'll misconstrue that as him placing a cuckold's horns upon my brow.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his future misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Uses unexpectedly sophisticated language for adultery anxiety
jokes.explanation: Homer unexpectedly eloquent about being cuckolded, using archaic terms

jokes.text: Because you never feed him, walk him or let him out to pee.
jokes.setup: Homer asking why dog attacked him
jokes.punchline: Simple explanation of basic pet neglect
jokes.explanation: Straightforward explanation of Homer's terrible pet ownership

jokes.text: To a man I'm happy to call my best friend, Homer Simpsno! Simpson! Dyslexia, Duffman's secret shame.
jokes.setup: Duffman giving heartfelt toast
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces name, then reveals unexpected vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Humanizes corporate mascot character with unexpected personal struggle.

tmdb_overview: Homer loses his memory--and his family--when he drinks Moe's most potent libation, the Forget-Me-Shot, in an episode that spoofs "Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind" and "It's a Wonderful Life."
